name: LIPELLA PHARMACEUTICALS INC.
business: We are a clinical-stage biotechnology company focused on developing new drugs by reformulating the active agents in existing generic drugs and optimizing these reformulations for new applications. Our product candidates include LP-10 for hemorrhagic cystitis, LP-310 for oral lichen planus, LP-410 for oral graft-versus-host disease, and LP-50 for non-muscle invasive bladder cancer. We have not yet commercialized any products and do not expect to generate revenue from sales for several years.
sellsTo: patients with non-muscle invasive bladder cancer

exposures.sentence: We are a clinical-stage biotechnology company focused on developing new drugs by reformulating the active agents in existing generic drugs and optimizing these reformulations for new applications.

catalysts.event: Completion of Phase 2a clinical trial for LP-310
catalysts.sentence: The trial is expected to be completed in the second quarter of 2025.

catalysts.event: Podium presentation of top line data from first two dose cohorts of LP-310 Phase 2a trial at AAOM/EAOM Joint Meeting
catalysts.sentence: The top line data from the first two dose cohorts of this trial has been selected for podium presentation at the 2025 American Association of Oral Medicine and European Association of Oral Medicine Joint Meeting that will be held in Las Vegas, NV on May 15, 2025.

catalysts.event: Submission of Breakthrough Designation request for LP-310
catalysts.sentence: Lipella anticipates the submission to the FDA of a request for Breakthrough Designation for LP-310 in the second half of 2025.

tamClaims.claim: Potential annual gross revenue of up to $1.2 billion for LP-10
tamClaims.sentence: Based on such price and demand estimates, we believe there is potential to receive up to $1.2 billion in annual gross revenue.

tamClaims.claim: Total addressable market exceeding $980 million for LP-310
tamClaims.sentence: Given the absence of FDA approved treatment of OLP, we estimate revenue of approximately $4,000 per course of therapy, resulting in a total addressable market that exceeds $980 million.

tamClaims.claim: Addressable market in excess of 120,000 patients annually for LP-10
tamClaims.sentence: LP-10 has been developed to address this form of bleeding, as well as bladder bleeding associated with breast cancer patients who are taking systemic cyclophosphamide or ifosfamide, leading to chemotherapy-related cystitis experienced by an estimated 60,000 patients annually in the United States, inferring an addressable market in excess of 120,000 patients annually.
